What is the sum of the largest and the smallest 4-digit numbers made by using single digit prime numbers (without repetition)?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Largest number formed is 7532
Smallest number formed 2357
Their sum will be 9889

Q19 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) A priority queue in UCS stores nodes based on their cumulative cost.
(b) A higher branching factor in IDA* increases the number of nodes to explore, affecting its time complexity.
(c) Min-Max evaluates all moves to find the optimal one, assuming optimal play by the opponent.
(d) Greedy Best-First Search uses minimal memory by storing only the current node and itssuccessors.
Which of the following is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, C, D
Solution

(a, b, c, d)
• Uniform Cost Search (UCS) stores nodes in a priority queue ordered by their cumulative cost, ensuring the next node explored has the lowest cost. So, option (a) is correct.
• In Iterative Deepening A* (IDA*), a higher branching factor leads to a larger search space, increasing the number of nodes explored and affecting time complexity. So, option (b) is correct.
• Min-Max evaluates all possible moves to find the optimal move, assuming that both players will play optimally (minimizing and maximizing their respective outcomes). So, option (c) is correct.
• Greedy Best-First Search may use more memory, as it needs to store nodes in the frontier (queue), not just the current node and its successors. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q26 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : LOOCV leads to higher variance, especially when testing with outlier data points.
S2 : SVM uses the soft margin technique for outliers or non-separable data.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• LOOCV can have higher variance, especially with outliers, since each test set consists of only one data point, making the model sensitive to outliers. So, S1 is correct.
• SVM uses the soft margin technique, allowing some misclassification (outliers) by introducing a trade-off between margin size and classification errors. So, S2 is correct.

Q29 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is true about linked list implementation of queue?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
To keep the first in first out order a queue can be implemented using linked list in 2 ways.
1. If a new node is inserted at the beginning of linked list, then in dequeue operation node must be removed from end.
2. If new node is inserted at the end of linked list then, in dequeue operation, node must be removed from beginning.

Q36 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) In mean binning each bin’s value is replaced by the mean of all values in that bin.
(b) Median binning replaces the values in a bin with the median of the values within the bin.
(c) In equal-width binning, the data range is split into bins of equal size.
(d) Frequency binning divide data into bins with approximately equal number of data points.
Which of the above is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
• In mean binning, data is grouped by bins, and the mean is calculated for each bin, but the statement oversimplifies the process. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• Median binning replaces bin value with the median of the data points in the bin. So, option (b) is correct.
• Equal-width binning divides the data range into equal width interval, not equal numbers of data points. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Frequency binning ensure that each bin contains approximately the same number of data points. So, option (d) is correct.

Q40 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, C, D
Solution

(a, c, d)
• K-Means uses the mean of points in a cluster as the centroid, and this centroid may not necessarily be one of the actual data points. So, option (a) is correct.
• Multi-layer perceptrons (MLPs) are not limited to binary classification tasks. They can be used for multi-class classification as well, depending on the output layer and activation function used. So, option (b) is incorrect
• K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) is a lazy learning algorithm, meaning it does not require a training phase. The training phase is only used to store the data points, and predictions are made during the testing phase by comparing the test data to the stored points. So, option (c) is correct.
• Regression is a type of supervised learning used to predict continuous target variables, such as predicting house prices or stock values. So, option (d) is correct.
